other_observers: Joyce Takamine and Sandy Arnesen; We went based on Connie Kogler's reports in 2010 and 2011. We were unaware of this bird being reported yet this year.
specific_location: Tunnel Campground about 6 miles north of Hwy 14 on the Larimie River Road.
description: This bird came to a feeder in the company of Broad-tailed Hummingbirds. This made her large size quite obvious. The length of the bill and the thickness of the bill-base strongly contrasted with the bill length and thickness of the adjacent Broad-taileds’ bills. The bill was straight. She had a short, white post-ocular streak and dark auriculars. Below the auriculars was pale gray as was the speckled throat; the chest was a bit darker gray.  The top of head, back and tail were bronzy-green. The tail had small white corners.  We saw what we assume was the same bird earlier in the day while it was feeding facing us. The view showed a mottled chest and a bulky bird. The white tail corners were seen when she left.  Both views were briefBehaviors: It came to hummingbird feeders at the same location a female Mag had been reported the last two years.Call: none heardPlumage: adult female
